name: Employer provided earplugs but I didn't always wear them—can they deny my hearing claim?
description: No—protection factor but not absolute bar if harmful noise proven causal.[1]

mainEntity.name: Employer provided earplugs but I didn't always wear them—can they deny my hearing claim?

acceptedAnswer.text: No—protection factor but not absolute bar if harmful noise proven causal.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: No, provision doesn't auto-deny if noise was 'harmful' and you prove exposure caused loss; courts weigh actual heard noise.[1] Utah considers protection but doesn't bar claims.[1] Key factors: Show inconsistent use still led to damage, medical causation overrides.[1][5] Document noise levels, get expert to affirm.[1] Appeal denials with attorney.[5]
